cards.definition: able to recover quickly from difficulty; tough and flexible
cards.example: Coral reefs are surprisingly resilient and can regrow after damage.

cards.definition: to make something less severe, harmful, or painful
cards.example: Planting trees can help mitigate the effects of rising temperatures.

cards.definition: gases or other substances sent out into the air, especially harmful ones
cards.example: Countries agreed to cut carbon emissions over the next decade.

cards.definition: able to continue over a long time without harming the environment
cards.example: Solar power is a more sustainable source of energy than coal.

cards.definition: easily harmed, hurt, or affected by something
cards.example: Low-lying islands are especially vulnerable to rising sea levels.

cards.definition: to change your behaviour or ideas to fit a new situation
cards.example: Farmers must adapt to longer droughts and hotter summers.

cards.definition: not enough; hard to find because there is very little of it
cards.example: Fresh water is becoming scarce in many dry regions.

cards.definition: a result or effect of an action or situation
cards.example: Flooding is one consequence of extreme rainfall.

cards.definition: (of energy) naturally replaced and never running out
cards.example: Wind and solar are renewable sources of electricity.

cards.definition: to interrupt or disturb the normal course of something
cards.example: Storms can disrupt food supplies and transport.

cards.definition: the level or point at which something starts to happen
cards.example: Scientists warn we are near a dangerous warming threshold.

cards.definition: existing in large amounts; more than enough
cards.example: Sunlight is abundant in desert regions, making solar power ideal.

cards.definition: to use up or greatly reduce the amount of something
cards.example: Overfishing can deplete ocean populations within years.

cards.definition: to publicly support or recommend a particular idea or plan
cards.example: Many scientists advocate for a rapid shift to clean energy.
